eval_1_streaming_counter.key_finding: Simple streaming patterns are well-understood across all baselines

eval_2_greeting_form.key_finding: Canonical ZIO-wrapping error appears consistently without skill guidance

eval_3_live_search.key_finding: Signal-based patterns are well-understood across all baselines

eval_4_realtime_chat.key_finding: Complex multi-client patterns improve significantly with skill (37.5% improvement vs without-skill)

high_discrimination_assertions.canonical_error: ZIO-wrapping confusion between event {} and events {} wrappers

high_discrimination_assertions.description: Two-phase SSE: sends history first (Inner), then streams new messages (Append)
high_discrimination_assertions.canonical_error: Syntax errors and pattern confusion in multi-client broadcasting

low_discrimination_assertions.finding: Agents naturally understand events wrapper and ServerSentEventGenerator without guidance
Agents naturally understand Signal binding and debounce without guidance

anomalies.observation: Agent succeeds when no zio-http-* skills available, but fails when zio-http-knowledge skill is present. Suggests skill context or sampling variation. Possible first-principles reasoning advantage when domain-specific guidance is absent.
anomalies.recommendation: Run multiple replicates (3-5) to establish statistical confidence

canonical_errors.name: ZIO-Wrapping in Event Handlers
canonical_errors.symptom: Handler returns name.map { n => DatastarEvent.patchElements(...) } instead of DatastarEvent directly
canonical_errors.root_cause: Confusion between event { } (returns DatastarEvent) and events { } (returns ZIO). Agents default to wrapping in ZIO when uncertain.
canonical_errors.skill_prevention_method: Step 4 explicitly shows DatastarEvent returned directly with clear examples

canonical_errors.name: Multi-Client Broadcasting Pattern Errors
canonical_errors.symptom: Multiple errors: syntax in SSE handler, no readSignals[T], no signal binding, manual JavaScript initialization
canonical_errors.root_cause: Complex pattern combining service injection, type-safe form parsing, and two-phase SSE. Without pattern visibility, agents make multiple mistakes.
canonical_errors.skill_prevention_method: Common Patterns section with full ChatServer.scala example showing all patterns in context

baseline_definitions.with_skill: Agents have access to zio-http-datastar skill
baseline_definitions.without_skill: Agents have other zio-http-* skills (knowledge, scaffold) but not zio-http-datastar
baseline_definitions.without_all_skills: Agents have no zio-http-* skills; pure training data + reasoning

assertion_grading.pass_criteria: Code compiles, required patterns present, follows idiomatic Datastar/ZIO conventions
assertion_grading.fail_criteria: Compilation errors, missing required patterns, non-functional implementations
assertion_grading.note: Non-idiomatic but functional patterns (e.g., manual parsing vs readSignals[T]) count as pass

methodology.statistical_notes: Sample size: 1 run per baseline. Variance not measured. Future iterations should run 2-3 replicates to establish confidence intervals. Results may be influenced by sampling, model temperature, and prompt phrasing.

evidence: Prevents canonical ZIO-wrapping error consistently (100% prevention rate)
Improves complex pattern implementation by 37.5% vs without-skill
No false positives or downsides observed
Maintains high performance on simple patterns where no guidance needed
next_steps: Add skill to zio-skills package for production use
Consider running replicates (3-5 per baseline) to establish statistical confidence bounds
Plan follow-up skills for advanced patterns (streaming, middleware, authentication)
Gather real-world usage feedback to inform description optimization
